candidates.name: faster-whisper local transcription pipeline
candidates.detail: Complete local Whisper lifecycle: CUDA detection, large-v3 model, beam_size=10, vad_filter=True, segment iteration with tqdm, metadata capture (timestamps, avg_logprob). ~50 lines covering all operational edge cases.

candidates.name: Whisper tuned params (beam_size=10, vad_filter)
candidates.detail: beam_size=10 (vs default 5) for accuracy. vad_filter=True to skip silence and prevent Whisper hallucination-on-silence issue. These are tuned production params, not defaults.

candidates.name: Caption-first + Whisper-fallback architecture
candidates.detail: Insight from cross-repo analysis: both bedrock and this repo skip captions and go straight to transcription. pytubefix has yt.captions — use captions for ~80% of videos, Whisper only as fallback. Dramatically cheaper and faster.

candidates.name: Async download + sync GPU transcription pattern
candidates.detail: asyncio.Semaphore(4) for concurrent downloads, sequential GPU transcription. Correct architecture — network I/O parallelizes, GPU compute doesn't.

candidates.name: faster-whisper library reference
candidates.detail: CTranslate2 Whisper implementation. 4x faster than OpenAI whisper. GPU + CPU. The local transcription engine for T27.

candidates.name: CUDA/GPU detection + CPU fallback pattern
candidates.detail: numba.cuda.is_available() check, Anaconda toolkit path detection, float16 vs auto compute type selection. Real-world GPU setup complexity.

candidates.name: SpaCy + compromise.js two-stage NLP
candidates.detail: Server-side SpaCy for sentence splitting during transcription, client-side compromise.js for paragraph restructuring in reader. Two complementary NLP stages.

candidates.name: API vs local cost tradeoff ($0.006/min)
candidates.detail: OpenAI Whisper API: $0.006/min, less accurate. Local faster-whisper: free, more accurate, requires GPU. 1-hour video = $0.36 API vs $0 local. Concrete data for T27 architecture decision.

candidates.name: compromise.js client-side NLP
candidates.detail: 75KB client-side NLP. Sentence/paragraph detection in browser. 11K+ stars. Potential for SoNash frontend text processing.

candidates.name: Monolithic single-file tool anti-pattern
candidates.detail: 280 lines, no CLI, no config file, hard-coded vars. Works for personal use, impossible to integrate. T27 pipeline must be modular handlers.

candidates.name: Skip-captions-always anti-pattern
candidates.detail: Both bedrock and this repo transcribe every video from scratch. pytubefix has caption extraction. Check captions first — ~80% of YouTube videos have them. Don't Whisper what's already text.

candidates.name: Hard-coded source config anti-pattern
candidates.detail: Module-level variables for config including API keys. No CLI args, no env vars. Anti-twelve-factor.

cross_repo_connections.detail: Local Whisper vs AWS Transcribe. Both use pytubefix. Both skip captions. Together they map the full cloud-vs-local spectrum for T27. This repo is the local-first reference, bedrock is the managed-service reference.
AWS-MEF does frame extraction + visual analysis. This repo does audio extraction + Whisper transcription. Different media modalities for T27.
Second independent repo choosing pytubefix for YouTube download. Cross-validates library choice. Also uses Playlist class for batch processing.
Core transcription engine. Not analyzed yet — potential deep-dive target for T27.
